title: I Am a Lion
body:
I am the king—
At least, that's what the stories say.
The king of the jungle,
though my roots stretch
across the savanna.

My crown is my mane,
bejeweled with the wisdom
of the kings before me.
I carry the strength
of my people from Africa—
the rhythm of ancestors,
the heartbeat of my pride.

Yet perhaps it is a curse.
For it is my pride they target,
my very being they fear.

Poachers come with chains,
with bullets, with laws,
trying to strip me
of what makes me royal.

My boldness breeds bold hatred—
hatred for my skin,
hatred for my roar.

But I will not cease.
I will fight,
for my way of life,
for the legacy written
in every step of my stride.

I am a lion,
strong and proud,
unyielding in courage.

I am a lion.
